meta.title: Semantic Control — Vision vs. Reality
meta.description: Fidelity audit: JP's semantic-control vision (V1-V5) across PM corpus, canon/spec, and actual code

props.title: Semantic Control — Vision vs. Reality
props.subtitle: Five vision elements, measured across the PM corpus, the canon/spec, and the code that actually runs

props.content: The question wasn't "is intent-conservation wired" — it was whether the whole semantic-control idea exists anywhere: FlowMind-defined evals, auto-loading module evals, a default core-loop evaluator, overlay attachment, and LLM-as-judge with core/eval owning the score. The answer: it's *planned across dozens of artifacts and four workstreams*, *partially canonized*, *barely built*, and the live runtime *actively violates the core principle* by letting the agent grade itself.

props.label: AT A GLANCE
props.title: Where the five elements stand in the running code

props.label: Fully built in the live path
THE THESIS
props.title: Planned everywhere, built nowhere, violated live
The gap is implementation + fragmentation, not design
props.content: Semantic control is one of the most heavily-represented ideas in the project — a dedicated `semantic-control-engine` workstream with ~10 captures, `eval-framework-1-0`, `intent-governance`, ~30 eval/intent/proof tasks, a dozen designs, six docs, three DNA files. The canon even nails the hardest principle: **core/eval is the only scorer; the LLM owns no math and no lifecycle decision.** But two problems: the vision is **fragmented** across four workstreams (mostly captures and drafts, rarely crisp acceptance criteria), and the **code lags far behind** — the intent-conservation evaluator is **orphaned** (zero production callers), overlay-merge **does not exist** in the executor, module auto-load is **absent**, there is **no** core/eval LLM-judge capability — and the live `lev.evaluate.semantic_control` gate **JSON-parses the agent's own `passed` boolean and trusts it.** The agent grades itself, the exact failure the one-brain doctrine exists to kill.

props.label: THE MATRIX
props.title: V1–V5 across all three surfaces
props.subtitle: Green = real / Amber = partial / Red = stub, absent, orphaned, or violated. The pattern: PM & canon ahead, code behind.

rows: V1 · Evals are FlowMind-defined, ~95% no code
Foundation real (contract+registry+parser); semantic-novelty is the template but only in TESTS. No production no-code authoring surface; "hundreds/95%" is not a measurable criterion anywhere.
V2 · <module>/flows + /evals auto-load

Eval roots spec'd (eval.dna + eval-framework); paired flows+evals is NOT one contract, and discovery conventions contradict across 3 docs. Code: only plugin config-discovery; no fractal module loader.
V3 · intent-conservation = default core-loop eval

Canon emphatic ("first stock evaluator, never hardcoded"). PM: planned as first bundled eval but still fixture/warn-only in places. Code: evaluateIntentConservation has ZERO production callers; live path uses a command-string JSON hook.
V4 · Evals attach as always-on overlay

PM: FlowMind overlays the core loop & inherits evidence; no explicit "always-on, never re-declared" contract. Canon has overlay vocab. Code: overlay-merge "does not exist" — metadata + a CLI flag only.
V5 · No self-grading; LLM judges, core/eval scores

PM & canon strong: core/eval sole scorer, LLM owns no math/lifecycle (*the exact "LLM doesn't do the math" boundary isn't crisply stated). Code: NO core/eval LLM-judge (type label + plugin-local ERC only); dup_judge is a sensor stub; the self-grading JSON hook is the AUTHORITATIVE path.

props.label: HOW WELL REPRESENTED?
props.title: The five representation gaps
props.subtitle: Where the vision has no crisp home, even though it's discussed widely

rows: V1's "hundreds of evals / 95% no code" is not a measurable acceptance criterion anywhere
The scale ambition that justifies FlowMind-defined evals is aspirational prose, not a gate
V2 has no single fractal auto-discovery contract for paired <module>/flows + /evals
Eval roots are spec'd, FlowMind roots are scattered across 3 conflicting conventions — can't build the loader until one is chosen
V3/V4 are split across 4 workstreams (semantic-control-engine, eval-framework, core-module-audit, proof-spine)
Intent-conservation's ownership is fragmented; PM still describes warn-only/fixture states in several places
semantic-novelty has no standalone PM home saying "defines its own eval while IC stays cross-cutting"
The canonical example of the overlay+node split isn't pinned as a reference
V5's LLM-as-judge boundary isn't named exactly
PM/canon are directionally right but never say crisply: judge gets rubric+vars, core/eval computes the score, self-report never grades

props.title: 🚨 The live gate self-grades
props.content: `exec-local.ts:272` fires only when `command === 'lev.evaluate.semantic_control'`; `:304` `parseSemanticControlEvaluation` JSON-parses the adapter's output and trusts `proof.passed`. No evaluator runs — the agent reports its own verdict, then it's relabeled `gate_id: semantic_control, driver: intent-conservation`. This is the false-green the cutover exists to eliminate, sitting in the live path.

props.title: The real evaluator is orphaned
props.content: `evaluateIntentConservation` (`intent-conservation.ts:47`) computes drift + thresholds — and is called **only from tests and exports**. G4's generic comparator `discharge()` has exactly one caller (`legacy-redischarger.ts`). The machinery you designed exists and passes its tests; the runtime quietly bypasses it.

props.title: Overlay-merge does not exist
props.content: `sdlc-tick-overlay.flow.yaml:40` says it outright: the executor mechanism is unbuilt. There's overlay vocabulary, an index pass, and a `--flowmind-overlay` CLI flag — but no graph injection. So "intent-conservation always-on, never re-declared per loop" is not achievable today; the executor only runs explicit nodes.

props.title: Module auto-load is absent
props.content: No `<module>/flows + <module>/evals` fractal loader. The closest is `discoverPluginFlowConfigs` (`plugin-config-discovery.ts:40`) which scans `plugins/*/config.yaml` for hand-registered `flowmind.programs`. Evals run via explicit `lev eval run <suite-path>`. The eval-side roots are *spec'd* in DNA but not implemented as drop-in auto-discovery.

props.title: No core/eval LLM-as-judge
props.content: `'llm-as-judge'` is a type label in `scorecard-registry.ts:4` — no runtime path prompts an LLM with a methodology and lets core/eval own the score. The real LLM-judge lives plugin-local in ERC (`prompt-testing.ts:74`) and autoresearch (heuristic default). `dup_judge` in the novelty fixture is a `sensor.categorical` fed from fixture data, not a judge. The pattern you want — "judge using these vars + methodology, core/eval scores" — exists only as scattered ingredients.

props.label: THE PM / CANON CORPUS
props.title: It's vast — and that's the paradox
props.subtitle: Documented and planned far more thoroughly than it is built

props.label: CONTRADICTIONS TO RESOLVE
props.title: Where the canon disagrees with itself
props.subtitle: These are decisions, not bugs — they block a clean implementation

rows: Flow discovery convention
flowmind.dna: entity-local → .lev/flows → plugins/*/flows
ARCHITECTURE: src/flowmind/*.flow.yaml; eval.dna: */evals/**/*.eval.yaml
Pick ONE discovery contract before V2 auto-load can be built
Does the LLM score?
graph.yaml:267 — LLM produces variable scores + confidence
spec:763 — LLM returns observations ONLY; code scores
This IS V5: judge emits typed observations, core/eval computes the score
Is intent-conservation always-on?
gates: every admitted iteration MUST prove conservation
gates expose intent_conservation.enabled:false; design says default needs config-policy resolution
Decide the always-on overlay semantics + the disable contract

props.label: WHAT THE WORK ACTUALLY IS
props.title: Wire the canon into code; kill the shortcut
The shape of the fix
props.content:
Because the canon is rich and the foundation (EvaluatorContract, registry, discharge, the novelty fixture) is real, the work is *connection*, not *design*:

**1.** Route the core loop's intent-conservation through the same `measure → discharge` registry path the novelty fixture already uses, fed by real evidence — and **delete the self-grading JSON hook**. (Resolves V3 + V5-live + the naming split in one move.)
**2.** Add an LLM-as-judge **capability** in core/eval: judge gets vars + methodology, returns typed observations, core/eval thresholds. (Lands V5 as designed; unifies the scattered ERC/autoresearch judges.)
**3.** Build the `<module>/flows + /evals` auto-loader against ONE resolved discovery convention. (Lands V2; resolves contradiction #1.)
**4.** Build executor overlay-merge so intent-conservation is always-on without per-loop redeclaration. (Lands V4.)
**5.** Promote semantic-novelty from test fixture to the canonical no-code eval template + a production eval registry. (Lands V1.)
**6.** Consolidate the four fragmented workstreams' semantic-control scope into one owned plan with the V1 scale criterion and the V5 judge-boundary stated crisply. (Closes the representation gaps.)

None of this is "figure out semantic control" — that's done, on paper. It's "make the running code do what the canon already says."
